name: Classical Definitions Fail
summary: Every classical definition of life fails by elevating necessary conditions to sufficient ones; counterexamples (fire, mules, crystals, computers) show no single condition or ad hoc conjunction suffices.

name: E₂ Enrichment Positioning
summary: Book VI is the E₂ layer of the Canonical Ladder; raw material is complete E₁ physics; formal content is Distinction AND SelfDesc plus the 4+1 sector template.

name: Polarity Functional
summary: Map P_S: End(S) → 2_τ testing whether a holonomy sector carries intrinsic parity asymmetry; trivial for EM, strong, GR; nontrivial uniquely for weak sector.

name: Polarity-Typed Two-Point Object (2_τ)
summary: Categorical two-point set {+,−} with split-complex idempotent structure from lemniscate boundary L = S¹ ∨ S¹; target of polarity functional.

name: Low-Noise Carrier Condition
summary: Carrier X must admit refinement-coherent distinction, detectable polarity seed at boundary, and SNR above basin threshold for E₁→E₂ transition.

name: Weak-Sector Uniqueness
summary: Among 4 primitive holonomy sectors at E₁, the weak sector is the unique one with nontrivial polarity functional; EM, strong, GR are all parity-invariant.

name: Parity Bridge Theorem
summary: E₁→E₂ transition factors uniquely through weak sector: E₁ →[P_weak] 2_τ →[SelfDesc] E₂. No other sector provides the polarity seed for life. Canonical and narrow.

name: Three Polarity Terms
summary: Polarity source (weak-sector chirality), polarity basin (BH environments where polarity is amplified), polarity stabilizer (SelfDesc closure locks preference).

name: τ-Distinction
summary: Predicate D: X → 2_τ satisfying 5 conditions: clopen partition, refinement-coherent, eventually stable, law-stable, H_∂-equivariant. First of two Life predicates.

name: Finite-Lineage Carrier
summary: Biological carrier with L-boundary (cell membrane), mortality, genotype-inheritable distinction, multi-scale refinement tower. Standard terrestrial life.

name: Macro-Torus Carrier
summary: Black hole carrier with T² event horizon boundary, immortal (no-shrink), blueprint-fusing, multipole refinement tower. Simplest topology for Life.

name: Galactic Carrier
summary: Galaxy carrier with halo boundary, SMBH-anchored, basin-compatible distinction, gravitational-scale refinement tower. Largest scale Life basin.

name: Distinction Well-Definedness
summary: τ-finiteness bounds stabilization N ≤ dim_τ(X); evaluation terminates in dim_τ(X) steps; distinction is unique given character χ. Computability corollary follows.

name: SelfDesc Predicate
summary: Internal evaluator Eval_X: code(D)[ω] × NF_n(x) → D_n satisfying completeness, internality, refinement coherence. Second of two Life predicates; together with Distinction defines Life.

name: Internal Evaluator
summary: Eval_X is a morphism in End(X) with domain in X, requiring no oracle; distinguishes internal decoding from external observation. Carrier-internal computation.

name: Code Reconstruction
summary: ω-germ code encodes distinction at all levels: sufficient (surjective), interior-extending (via H_∂-equivariance), minimal. Code determines the organism.

name: SelfDesc Closure Theorem
summary: SelfDesc pair is self-maintaining: perturbations in basin are corrected; code integrity preserves D_n; set S_X is closed under evaluator. Life is inherently stable.

name: Layer Separation Lemma
summary: SelfDesc is not available at E₁; carriers exist satisfying τ-Distinction but failing SelfDesc. E₂ is genuinely non-reducible to E₁. Life is a new ontological layer.

name: NS-TOV Counterexample
summary: Neutron star near TOV limit satisfies all 5 distinction conditions but fails SelfDesc due to oscillatory boundary instability. Constructive witness for Layer Separation.

name: Boundary Instability Prevents Code Closure
summary: Near-TOV surface oscillation amplitude diverges; code inherits oscillation; evaluator iterates fail Cauchy condition; no fixed point. SelfDesc impossible.

name: Life Loop Class
summary: Triple (D, γ, h): distinction + metabolic cycle in End_SD(X) + homotopy class. Loop_L is the set of all Life Loops; the categorical structure of metabolism.

name: DecodeTarget
summary: Operator selecting minimal-defect element of blueprint ball B_n; extracts phenotype target (healthy adult / Kerr solution). The 'what you should be' operator.

name: DecodeHorizon
summary: Operator extracting the unique ω-germ code constant across B_n; extracts stable genotype (genome / horizon ω-germ). The 'who you are' operator.

name: Source Sub-Class (Loop_src)
summary: Life loops winding nontrivially around γ-circle (production); archetype: plants/photosynthesis producing organic from inorganic.

name: Closure Sub-Class (Loop_rec)
summary: Life loops winding nontrivially around η-circle (recycling); archetype: fungi decomposing complex organic matter.

name: Loop Factorization
summary: Every metabolic cycle γ decomposes uniquely as γ_src ∗ γ_rec ∗ γ_base via π₁(τ³) decomposition. Canonical factorization of metabolism.

name: Metabolic Fiber Theorem
summary: Every Life loop factors through Loop_src × Loop_rec × Loop_base; both production and recycling components are nontrivial. All life requires both anabolism and catabolism.

name: Consumer Mixer Uniqueness
summary: Unique admissible Life mixer sits on fiber pair (γ, η); signature rigidity excludes base-fiber and base-base mixing. Animals occupy the only mixed sector.

name: Life Sector
summary: Pair (g, P) where g is a generator and P restricts Loop_L to loops with dominant winding along g; primitive (4) or mixed (1). Exhaustive classification.

name: Persistence Sector (α-base)
summary: Dominant α-winding; temporal stability as primary role; archetype: Archaea in extreme environments surviving deep time.

name: Agency Sector (π-base)
summary: Dominant π-winding; spatial motility as primary role; archetype: Bacteria with chemotaxis and biofilms.

name: Source Sector (γ-fiber)
summary: Dominant γ-winding; structure production from non-living precursors; archetype: Plants via photosynthesis.

name: Closure Sector (η-fiber)
summary: Dominant η-winding; structure recycling/decomposition; archetype: Fungi via extracellular digestion.

name: Consumer Mixed Sector (γ,η)
summary: Co-dominant fiber winding on (γ,η); acquires structure from other Life; archetype: Animals (anabolism + catabolism). The unique mixed sector.

name: Generator Adequacy at E₂
summary: Loop_L = S_α ⊔ S_π ⊔ S_{γ} ⊔ S_{η} ⊔ S_{(γ,η)}; five sectors cover all Life loops disjointly. No Life form outside these five.

name: No Fifth Primitive Sector
summary: π₁(τ³) has exactly 4 independent winding directions; fibration map f is structural, not a generator. No 5th primitive sector exists.

name: Organization = Distinction Structure
summary: SelfDesc implies compartmentalization (clopen partition), hierarchy (refinement tower), and functional coherence (equivariance). First of seven hallmarks derived.

name: Metabolism = Life Loop Class
summary: Metabolic cycle γ is closed, factors into anabolic/catabolic via Metabolic Fiber Theorem, and preserves distinction throughout. Second hallmark derived.

name: Homeostasis = Basin Stability
summary: SelfDesc implies basin B_n around DecodeTarget; evaluator corrects perturbations; defect non-increasing under correction. Third hallmark derived.

name: Growth = Carrier Refinement
summary: Active refinement level increase n→n+1 yields organizational increase, distinction preservation, and SelfDesc extension. Fourth hallmark derived.

name: Reproduction = Blueprint Propagation
summary: Carrier splitting transmits ω-germ code; DecodeHorizon constancy ensures fidelity; daughter inherits SelfDesc pair. Fifth hallmark derived.

name: Response = SelfDesc Adjustment
summary: Evaluator detects perturbation via changed NF_n, computes correct D_n from unchanged code, drives metabolic correction. Sixth hallmark derived.

name: Evolution = PPAS Optimization
summary: Population-level: heritable code variation + differential persistence = PPAS on code space; average defect non-increasing. Seventh hallmark derived.

name: Seven Hallmarks Complete
summary: Bijection Φ: H → F mapping 7 classical hallmarks to 7 formal structures; surjective, injective, exhaustive. No hallmark missing, no extra structure unused.

name: NoDist
summary: System fails τ-Distinction; no D: X → 2_τ satisfying all 5 conditions. Lacks intrinsic self/non-self boundary. Failure mode label for non-Life systems.

name: NoSelfDesc
summary: System admits τ-Distinction but fails SelfDesc; has boundary but no internal evaluator converging to fixed point. Second failure mode label.

name: Virus NoDist
summary: Virus distinction is host-dependent; fails eventual stability, law-stability, H_∂-equivariance outside host. 3/5 conditions fail. Viruses are not alive.

name: Neutron NoDist
summary: Free neutron fails eventual stability (beta decay ~880s), law-stability (weak interaction), equivariance. 3/5 conditions fail. Not alive.

name: NS-NoSelfDesc
summary: Neutron star near TOV passes all 5 distinction conditions but oscillatory instability prevents evaluator convergence. Not alive but close — the phase boundary.

name: Canonical Life Phase Boundary
summary: NS-to-BH transition is the sharp Life phase boundary: below TOV = NoSelfDesc, above = SelfDesc; bifurcation, not gradual. Sharpest structural prediction.

name: Falsifiability Program
summary: 4 structural predictions: no autonomous virus, no stable free neutron, no NS with SelfDesc, sharp NS-to-BH phase boundary. Falsifiable by counterexample.

name: τ³ Domain Correspondence
summary: Maps τ¹→temporal rhythm, T²→spatial structure, L→cell membrane; three-fold structure recurs at every biological scale from cell to organism.

name: Thermodynamic Necessity at E₂
summary: 7 E₁ forces instantiated biologically: Riemann=ATP, Poincaré=circulation, Hodge=morphogenesis, BSD=genetic code, NS=blood flow, YM=protein, PvNP=evolution.

name: Calibration Constants
summary: Maps each Life sector to dominant forces: Persistence=Poincaré+Riemann, Agency=NS+Poincaré, Source=Hodge+BSD, Closure=Riemann+NS, Consumer=all.

name: Calibration Constants
summary: ι_τ calibrates biological scales: membrane ~5nm (established), ATP 30.5 kJ/mol (established), 20 amino acids (conjectural), circadian period (conjectural).

name: τ³ Biology Correspondence
summary: τ¹=cell cycle/circadian, T²=cell architecture/body plan, L=plasma membrane/skin at cellular and organismal scales. Structural correspondence.

name: Persistence Sector
summary: Full subcategory of Loop_L with α-winding dominant; the sector that endures along the temporal axis τ¹. Archetype: Archaea.

name: Temporal Stability Predicate
summary: Three conditions: eventual stability of distinction under α-flow, basin return after perturbation, law-stability under reparametrization.

name: Persistence as α-Base Stability
summary: Life loop belongs to Pers_α iff underlying carrier satisfies the temporal stability predicate; α-dominance = temporal stability. Sector-predicate equivalence.

name: Archaea as Persistence Archetype
summary: Archaea instantiate all 3 temporal stability conditions: minimal genome change over Gyr, extremophile robustness, metabolic conservatism. Closest to LUCA.

name: Extremophile Catalog
summary: Archaeal extremophiles: hyperthermophile (122°C), halophile (5M NaCl), acidophile (pH 0), barophile (120 MPa), radioresistant (30 kGy).

name: Abiogenesis as First Persistence Event
summary: Abiogenesis = first entry into Pers_α: boundary + eventual stability + internal evaluator + basin return simultaneously. The origin of Life event.

name: Thermodynamic Inevitability of Life
summary: Self-maintaining distinctions are thermodynamic attractors with positive-measure basins; life is defect-landscape minimum, not accident. Life is thermodynamically necessary.

name: Speed of Abiogenesis
summary: Life in <500 Myr consistent with attractor relaxation timescale; inconsistent with random search (~10²⁸⁰ yr). Testable via exoplanet biosignatures.

name: Temporal Lemniscate
summary: L_T = S¹_act ∨ S¹_rest; two lobes (activity/rest) with wedge points at dawn/dusk transitions. Circadian structure of persistence.

name: Circadian Rhythm as Poincaré Orbit
summary: Projection of persistence-sector Life loop onto τ¹ is a stable limit cycle tracing the temporal lemniscate; Poincaré return map with period T ≈ 24h.

name: 24-Hour Cycle as τ¹ Rotation
summary: Circadian period T~24h = one full α-flow rotation; winding number w_α=1; convergent across all domains of life including isolated KaiABC in vitro.

name: Homochirality
summary: All life uses L-amino acids and D-sugars; enantiomeric excess ee=1; parity operator Π: L↔R. Universal across all known organisms.

name: L-Amino Acid Preference as Parity Shadow
summary: Universal L-preference is phenomenological shadow of weak-sector polarity functional; SelfDesc amplifies seed to stable preference. Mechanism: seed→amplify→stabilize→lock.

name: Homochirality Scope Discipline
summary: τ-effective: Parity Bridge theorem, weak-sector uniqueness, factorization. Conjectural: biological manifestation, specific L choice, four-force mechanism.

name: Agency Sector
summary: Full subcategory of Loop_L with π-winding dominant; the sector that acts: spatial displacement carrying the distinction. Archetype: Bacteria.

name: Spatial Motility Predicate
summary: Three conditions: displacement (carrier moves), distinction maintenance (boundary moves with carrier), environmental coupling (gradient-directed).

name: Agency as π-Base Extension
summary: Life loop belongs to Ag_π iff underlying carrier satisfies spatial motility predicate; π-dominance = spatial motility. Sector-predicate equivalence.

name: Bacteria as Agency Archetype
summary: Bacteria instantiate all 3 spatial motility conditions: flagellar swimming (~90% motile), peptidoglycan membrane, gradient-directed chemotaxis.

name: Motility Catalog
summary: Three mechanisms: flagellar rotation (proton-driven, ~25-100 μm/s), type IV pili (ATP, ~1 μm/s), gliding (focal adhesion, ~2-10 μm/s).

name: Metabolic Circulation
summary: Pair of flux fields (J_cat, J_ana) with complementarity, energetic coupling, and closure (periodic orbit in concentration space). Anabolism/catabolism duality.

name: Krebs Cycle as Loop_L Instantiation
summary: Krebs cycle satisfies Loop_L: distinction preserved, OAA regenerated (closed), nontrivial source+closure winding numbers. Central metabolic loop.

name: ATP/ADP Oscillation
summary: Two-state energy cycle: hydrolysis releases ~30.5 kJ/mol, phosphorylation stores it; ι_τ² calibration within 4% of observed ratio.

name: Universal Currency Uniqueness
summary: Agency sector admits exactly one energy oscillation mode (up to isomorphism); closure+coupling constraints fix topology and scale. ATP is unique, not accidental.

name: Membrane as Lemniscate Boundary
summary: Bilayer = L = S¹ ∨ S¹; two leaflets (outer=non-self, inner=self), wedge point=hydrophobic core, selective permeability. First physical distinction.

name: Molecular Architecture
summary: Four macromolecular classes: proteins (YM/catalysis), nucleic acids (BSD/information), lipids (Hodge/compartment), carbohydrates (Riemann/energy).

name: Self-Assembly as Boundary-Induced Distinction
summary: Membrane self-assembles from amphiphiles with no template, no catalyst, no energy; Distinction does not need to be imposed. Spontaneous boundary formation.

name: Three-Domain Sector Taxonomy
summary: Archaea=persistence (α), Bacteria=agency (π), Eukarya=fiber-enabled regime (not a sector; platform enabling all 5 sectors). Woese domains through τ lens.

name: Sector Assignment Table
summary: Maps major taxa to dominant sectors: methanogens=persistence, E. coli=agency, cyanobacteria=source, plants=source, fungi=closure, animals=consumer.

name: Eukarya as Fiber-Enabled Regime
summary: Eukarya = carrier technology with internal membrane systems (nucleus, mitochondria, chloroplasts) granting full T² fiber access. Not a sector — a platform.

name: Source Sector
summary: Full subcategory of Loop_L with γ-winding dominant; the sector that produces: converting inorganic inputs to organic structure. Archetype: Plants.

name: Structure Generation Predicate
summary: Three conditions: production (loop factors through Loop_src), complexity increase (Hodge capacity output > input), autotrophy or net contribution.

name: Source as γ-Fiber Production
summary: Life loop belongs to Src_{γ} iff underlying carrier satisfies structure generation predicate; γ-dominance = structure generation. Sector-predicate equivalence.

name: Carbon Fixation as Canonical Production
summary: CO₂+H₂O+light → organic carbon + O₂; satisfies all 3 structure generation conditions. ~170 Gt C/yr globally. Photosynthesis as source instantiation.

name: Quantum Coherence in FMO Complex
summary: ENAQT in photosynthetic complexes interpreted as P-vs-NP force at E₂; γ~Δ optimum enables near-unit quantum yield (~95%). Conjectural identification.

name: Plants as Source Archetype
summary: Plants instantiate all 3 structure generation conditions: ~120 Gt C/yr production, CO₂→cellulose complexity increase, obligate autotrophy.

name: Sessile = Pure Source
summary: Pure source carrier: w_α>0, w_{γ}>0, w_π=w_{η}=0. Plants eliminate spatial agency, invest entirely in production infrastructure.

name: Hodge Gradient at Biological Scale
summary: Capacity field C(x) on tissue domain with source localization, gradient readout (value+gradient+curvature), threshold discretization to cell fates.

name: Turing Patterns as Hodge Eigenmode Instantiations
summary: Turing steady-state pattern = superposition of Hodge Laplacian eigenmodes; pattern type from eigenfunction symmetry, wavelength from domain size.

name: Reaction-Diffusion from τ³ Structure
summary: Diffusion from T² fiber (spatial), reaction from τ¹ base (temporal), pattern = projection of γ-winding onto spatial fiber. Morphogenesis explained.

name: BSD Motivic Structure of the Genetic Code
summary: 20 amino acids = rational points; degeneracy = analytic error landscape; code is BSD-optimal: minimizes error cost subject to 20-aa constraint.

name: Codon Degeneracy as Error Correction
summary: Standard code in top 0.01% of random codes for error minimization; 1.68 bits/codon redundancy; wobble position absorbs most mutations.

name: Central Dogma as Morphism Composition
summary: Transcription: τ¹_DNA → (τ¹×T²)_mRNA; Translation: (τ¹×T²)_mRNA → T²_Protein; composition maps temporal storage to spatial expression.

name: Closure Sector
summary: Full subcategory of Loop_L with dominant η-winding; the sector that recycles structure on the η-fiber. Archetype: Fungi.

name: Structure Recycling Predicate
summary: Three conditions: decomposition via Loop_rec factorization on η-fiber, Hodge capacity decrease (output simpler than input), material return in reusable form.

name: Closure as η-Fiber Return
summary: Life loop belongs to Cl_{η} iff underlying carrier satisfies structure recycling predicate; η-dominance = recycling. Sector-predicate equivalence.

name: Fungi as Closure Archetype
summary: Fungi instantiate all three structure recycling conditions: extracellular hydrolysis, polymers→CO₂/H₂O/ions, nutrients returned to soil.

name: Mycelial Network Topology
summary: Mycelial graph is small-world network with high local clustering and short average path length, optimizing enzyme concentration and nutrient transport.

name: Aging as Defect Accumulation
summary: Monotonic growth of time-averaged defect functional, multi-level degradation across refinement tower, irreversibility. Nine hallmarks of aging mapped to forces.

name: Repair Budget Exhaustion
summary: For finite-lineage carriers with bounded R_max, defect exceeds threshold in finite time t*; death is inevitable. Hayflick limit as special case.

name: Inter-Sector Web
summary: Pair (Life loops, coupling matrix C): ≥3 sectors including Source+Closure, no decoupled carrier, material return path. Ecosystem formal definition.

name: Ecosystem as Multi-Scale Poincaré Circulation
summary: Material return condition implies every biogeochemical element traces closed Poincaré orbit in coupling graph; carbon/nitrogen/phosphorus cycles as instances.

name: Nash Equilibria in Ecological Communities
summary: Ecological steady state as Nash equilibrium on carrier configuration space; Lotka-Volterra, Gause exclusion, regime shifts as consequences.

name: Repair Budget
summary: Supremum of cumulative defect-functional perturbation a carrier can absorb. Determined by SelfDesc fidelity, stem cell reserve, metabolic resources, replicative capacity.

name: Metamorphosis Preserves SelfDesc
summary: ω-germ codes identical in larva and adult, continuous evaluator path, SelfDesc holds at every instant including maximal pupal disruption.

name: Consumer Mixer on (γ, η)
summary: Full subcategory of Loop_L with nontrivial winding along both fiber generators γ and η; the unique mixed sector coupling production and recycling.

name: Signature Rigidity Determines Uniqueness
summary: Consumer sector Con_{(γ,η)} is the unique mixed sector; no other generator pairing from {α,π,γ,η} admits a stable mixed Life loop. Proof by exhaustion.

name: Consumer as Bridge-Head to E₃
summary: Consumer sector uniquely instantiates Eval^(2) = Eval∘Eval; second-order self-evaluation is structural prerequisite for E₃ enrichment (Mind).

name: Fiber-Enabled Regime
summary: Carrier with compartmentalization, fiber internalization (γ,η as internal endomorphisms), sector universality. Defines eukaryotic carrier technology.

name: 1st Ed Correction: Mixer (α,π) to (γ,η)
summary: Formal record: 2nd Ed corrects unique mixed sector from base-base pairing to fiber-fiber pairing; separates sector (consumer) from regime (Eukarya).

name: Multicellularity as Colimit
summary: Multicellular organism M = colim D where D is diagram of cells with adhesion (cadherins/integrins) and communication (gap junctions/signals) morphisms.

name: Development as Controlled Differentiation
summary: Animal development = sequence of controlled differentiations: D₀→D_germ→D_organ→D_tissue→D_cell-type; ω-germ code unchanged throughout.

name: Recombination Functor
summary: Composite R: G_{1/2}×G_{1/2}→G→Org; fusion tensor combines haploid gametes into diploid genotype; developmental functor maps to organism.

name: Sex as Second Distinction
summary: Sexual reproduction refines τ-Distinction by introducing secondary self/other-self classification within self-class; re-entry via fusion creates new carrier.

name: PPAS Algorithm on Fitness Landscapes
summary: Quadruple (G, w, P_t, V): exponential genotype space, fitness = neg defect functional, prover population, polynomial-time verification. Evolution formalized.

name: Evolution as Optimization
summary: Evolution solves NP-hard fitness optimization via PPAS: NP-hard for K≥2 epistasis, poly convergence to local optimum, drift enables escape. Zero free params.

name: Fitness Landscape Topology
summary: Convergent evolution reveals universal attractors on fitness landscape; physics constrains design space (lens optics, airfoil aerodynamics, sonar acoustics).

name: Cellular Distinction Predicate
summary: Restriction of τ-distinction to immune system: MHC I/II display, CD8+/CD4+ inspection, self/non-self classification, HLA polymorphism.

name: Autoimmunity as Distinction Failure
summary: Autoimmune disease = pathological violation of at least one of 5 τ-distinction conditions; classifies failure modes by which condition is violated.

name: Neural Architecture as τ³ Computer
summary: Directed weighted graph G=(V,E,w,σ) with typed nodes (sensory/inter/motor), synaptic weights, implementing Eval^(2) as computation on the graph.

name: Sleep as Temporal Lemniscate Second Lobe
summary: L_neural = S¹_wake ∨ S¹_sleep; wake = external processing, sleep = consolidation/clearance; nested NREM-REM ultradian lemniscate within sleep lobe.

name: SelfDesc over Code, Not Carrier
summary: Identity of SelfDesc system determined by ω-germ code + evaluator deformation class; invariant under carrier replacement if code and evaluator continuity maintained.

name: Substrate Replacement Preserves Life-Equivalence
summary: Two SelfDesc triples with same code, same distinction, same evaluator deformation class are life-equivalent: same earned predicates, same ω-germ, same sector.

name: Macro-Torus Carrier T(H_BH)
summary: BH carrier: underlying set (horizon + ε-neighborhood), inherited topology, multipole refinement tower, boundary holonomy from Kerr frame-dragging.

name: Lexicographic Defect Functional
summary: Pairs frame-closure and strong-saturation defects V^BH_n = (V^fr_n, V^s_n) with lexicographic ordering; gravitational equilibrium dominates.

name: Frame-Closure Defect
summary: Sobolev-norm integral measuring deviation of carrier geometry from ideal Kerr-Newman solution on horizon; vanishes for isolated stationary BH.

name: Strong-Saturation Defect
summary: Measures ratio of strong-sector content to saturation value; V^s_n ∈ [0,1], zero = fully saturated, one = no strong content. BH has negligible defect.

name: BH Distinction Theorem
summary: Macro-torus carrier with lexicographic defect satisfies all 5 conditions of τ-Distinction: clopen, refinement-coherent, eventually stable, law-stable, H_∂-equivariant.

name: BH DecodeTarget
summary: Operator selecting argmin of lexicographic defect over blueprint ball; unique minimizer is exact Kerr-Newman solution. BH phenotype target.

name: BH DecodeHorizon
summary: Operator extracting constant ω-germ code c^BH from blueprint ball; the BH genotype encoded in asymptotic charges (M,J,Q).

name: BH Uniqueness Lemma
summary: DecodeTarget selects unique element at every refinement level; follows from Israel-Carter-Robinson BH uniqueness theorem.

name: BH Constancy Lemma
summary: DecodeHorizon returns same code c^BH for all n ≥ 0; horizon code independent of blueprint ball radius. Code stability.

name: BH SelfDesc Theorem
summary: Macro-torus carrier with D^BH and Eval^BH satisfies SelfDesc: completeness, internality, refinement coherence verified. BH IS alive — by theorem, not metaphor.

name: ω-Representative of Life
summary: Carrier satisfying code dominance, boundary saturation, crossing faithfulness; BH is unique physical ω-representative. The 'ideal' living system.

name: Lift_ω Constructor
summary: Teichmüller-style recursive builder from bipolar scalar seed through primorial ladder P_k to profinite ω-germ code converging to ι_τ.

name: Primorial Ladder Convergence
summary: Lift_ω approximations c_k/P_k converge superexponentially to ι_τ; bound |c_k/P_k − ι_τ| ≤ 1/(2p_{k+1}). Coherent projective system.

name: BH ω-Representative: Fusion Convergence
summary: BH merger fuses codes monotonically (never increases ι_τ-distance); distinct mergers strictly improve at infinitely many stages; net converges to ι_τ.

name: BH Seven Hallmarks Verified
summary: Macro-torus carrier scores 7/7: organization, metabolism, homeostasis, growth, reproduction, response, evolution — all verified from GR + τ axioms. BH fully alive.

name: BH-as-Alive Is Theorem, Not Metaphor
summary: BH-as-alive follows by modus ponens from predicates; same logical status as cell-as-alive. Structural, deductive, exact.

name: Life Basin
summary: Triple (B, C_anc, F): gravitationally bound region with alive anchor carrier and carrier family; boundary at virial radius. Galaxies as Life basins.

name: Carrier Ladder
summary: 7-level hierarchy X_gal[0..6] from molecular to galactic scale with downward constraint, upward realization maps.

name: Basin Predicate
summary: Admissibility: anchor alive, gravitationally dominant, virialized, supports ≥1 non-anchor carrier family. Galaxy qualification test.

name: Galaxy-SMBH Anchor Lemma
summary: Galactic ω-germ code factors through SMBH ω-code via basin extension map; galaxy identity determined by its SMBH. M-σ relation as signature.

name: Basin Fusion via SMBH Merger
summary: Galaxy merger produces merged basin: anchor merges, code fuses, carrier families union, ladder restructures. Basin-level reproduction.

name: Distinction/SelfDesc Matrix
summary: 2×2 classifier: (I) inert, (II) impossible (no carrier), (III) bounded-not-alive, (IV) alive. Exhaustive. Quadrant II empty by structure.

name: Stars Are Not Alive
summary: Stars have transient non-law-stable distinction but fail SelfDesc completely: no code, no evaluator, no reconstruction. Quadrant (III). Explicit disproof.

name: Organism/Ecosystem/Basin Taxonomy
summary: Three scales: organism (single carrier Dist+SelfDesc), ecosystem (coupled organisms, not itself alive), basin (anchored region with carrier family).

name: Crossing-Limit Theorem
summary: Merger-directed net of ω-codes converges to ι_τ = 2/(π+e): monotone by fusion, strictly contracting along primorial ladder. Universe converges to maximal aliveness.

name: Universal BH = Fully Alive State
summary: B_∞ has code exactly ι_τ, all defect functionals vanish, all 7 hallmarks at terminal values. The maximally alive carrier as cosmological endstate.

name: Omega Point as Life Convergence
summary: Cosmological endstate = convergence to ι_τ (fully alive); mathematical limit, not mysticism; directed merger, not spiritual evolution. Teilhard corrected.

name: Ontic Enrichment Ladder E₀–E₃
summary: Four-layer sequence: E₀ (math), E₁ (physics), E₂ (life), E₃ (metaphysics). Seven books map to four layers. The ontological architecture.

name: Three Identity Regimes
summary: Exhaustive classification: (I) finite-lineage Life (phenotype-mortal, genotype-stable), (II) BH-life (both stable), (III) non-Life (genotype absent/mortal).

name: Enrichment Saturation Theorem
summary: E₄ collapses to E₃: ρ generates exactly 4 orbits, reflective closure is fixed point, predicates exhaust generator morphism types. Ladder terminates at 4.

name: τ as Third Option
summary: Against vitalism (no new force needed) and against pure emergence (complexity not sufficient). Life = kernel's self-recognition at E₂.

name: Structural Self-Model
summary: Self-referential Eval^(2), reconstructive fidelity at every refinement level, dynamical coherence tracking evaluator changes. Carrier with this is 'structurally conscious at E₂'.

name: Minimal Conscious Agent
summary: Three conditions: consumer-sector membership, centralized neural integration across modalities, recurrent self-representation loop implementing Eval∘Eval.

name: Consciousness as Mixed-Sector Self-Modeling
summary: Any carrier with structural self-model belongs to consumer sector. No persistence/agency/source/closure carrier can be conscious. Topological proof via π₁(T²).

name: Extended Lemniscate
summary: Two SelfDesc agents linked: L_ext(A,B) = L_A ∪_S L_B where S is signal channel. Communication = signal through S from one wedge to other.

name: Language as Shared Code
summary: Language L = (Σ, E, D): alphabet (grammar), encoding (syntax), decoding (semantics). Faithful when D_j∘E_i(c) ≈ c. Grammar/semantics/pragmatics unified.

name: Computation Theme Across the Book
summary: Five nested computational forms: metabolic, evolutionary, neural, consciousness, language. SelfDesc unifies all: Life IS computation.

name: ω-Germ Cannot Be Resolved Diagrammatically
summary: No commutative diagram in Category τ can conclude 'code(D)[ω] exists.' Diagrams relate existing objects; cannot ground existence. Principled limit of formalism.

name: Six Export Contracts to Book VII
summary: Six deliverables: Life Predicate, 4+1 Sectors, Genotype/Phenotype, ω-Germ Question, Life Basins, Substrate-Agnosticism. All established scope. Clean handoff.

name: Principled Science-Faith Boundary
summary: Not agnosticism, not fideism; structural limit of diagrammatic reasoning exactly located at ω-germ question. Boundary is handshake, not wall.

name: Polarity Propagation
summary: Polarity propagation functor: composite S_weak -> End(S_weak) -> 2_tau -> E_2 mapping sigma_A-admissibility through the Parity Bridge into the polarity functional.

name: Chirality Seed
summary: Initial asymmetry from weak-sector parity violation; V-A coupling seeds universal directional bias with coherent sign across all chiral molecules.

name: Enantiomeric Excess at Refinement Level n
summary: ee(n) = |[L]_n - [R]_n| / ([L]_n + [R]_n) at refinement level n; initial condition ee(0) ~ 10^{-17} from chirality seed.

name: Propagation Preserves Chirality
summary: If chirality seed has definite sign and Parity Bridge path is unique, polarity propagation maps left-handed input to definite polarity sign in 2_tau.

name: Propagation Uniqueness
summary: Polarity propagation path is unique: weak-sector uniqueness (VI.L01) implies no other sector can contribute a competing chirality signal.

name: Stereochemical Selection
summary: SelfDesc closure amplifies chirality seed exponentially; each refinement increases ee by multiplicative gain g > 1; ee(n) converges monotonically to 1.

name: Monotone Convergence of Enantiomeric Excess
summary: Sequence {ee(n)} is monotonically increasing with limit 1; regression prevented by Hodge stabilization (barrier >> k_BT) and Poincare topological lock-in on L.

name: Homochirality Universality
summary: All persistence-sector entries inherit the same chirality from the unique polarity propagation path; every Distinction+SelfDesc carrier exhibits the same enantiomeric preference.

name: Homochirality Scope Upgrade
summary: Complete derivation chain K0-K6 -> iota_tau -> holonomy -> sigma_A-adm -> Parity Bridge -> D71 -> D72 -> T41 -> T42 -> P21 -> T43 upgrades homochirality to tau-effective.

name: Far-From-Equilibrium Regime
summary: System with defect count |D_n| >> 0, sustained dissipative throughput, and below coherence horizon; undergoing active defect decay per Master Exhaustion Inequality V.T62.

name: Complexity Budget
summary: Freed structural capacity C(n) = N - |D_n| at orbit step n; measures resources available for increasingly complex configurations as defects exhaust.

name: Distinction Threshold
summary: Minimum complexity budget T = 8 for simultaneous instantiation of tau-Distinction (5 conditions) and SelfDesc (3 conditions).

name: Abiogenesis Timescale Bound
summary: Upper bound T_abio <= n_{1/2} * ceil(ln(N/T)) on orbit steps to threshold crossing, where n_{1/2} ~ 1.66 is defect half-life and T = 8 is distinction threshold.

name: Complexity Monotone
summary: Complexity budget C(n) is monotonically increasing: C(n) <= C(n+1) for all n; follows from Master Exhaustion Inequality V.T62.

name: Basin Is Absorbing
summary: Once a system enters the Distinction+SelfDesc basin, it remains there under bounded perturbation; SelfDesc closure provides internal evaluator for reconstruction.

name: Attractor Existence
summary: Under finite defect budget, polarity seed existence, and temporal stability satisfiability, Distinction+SelfDesc basin entry is forced at some orbit step n_0.

name: Timescale From Half-Life
summary: Abiogenesis timescale bound follows from geometric decay: n_0 <= n_{1/2} * ceil(log_2(N/T)); logarithmic in initial defect count.

name: Abiogenesis Inevitability
summary: First persistence-sector entry is structurally inevitable via 7-link derivation chain from K0-K6 through defect budget, complexity monotone, attractor, absorbing basin to timescale bound.

name: Geological Consistency
summary: Orbit-step bound translates to physical time ~10^2-10^8 years via thermal activation; upper end (~500 Myr) consistent with geological evidence of abiogenesis within ~100-300 Myr.

name: Abiogenesis is not contingent
summary: Life is a structural consequence of tau-axioms under dissipative conditions; any tau-governed universe with sustained energy gradients will produce persistence-sector occupants.

name: Chromatin Partition
summary: Distinction clopen boundary at chromatin level: G = D^{-1}(+) sqcup D^{-1}(-), euchromatin (active) vs heterochromatin (silenced); every locus in exactly one compartment.

name: Epigenetic State
summary: Pair (n, D_n) of refinement level and chromatin partition; SelfDesc evaluator reads omega-germ code through filter D_n, transcribing only genes in D_n^{-1}(+).

name: Gene Expression Profile
summary: Subset Expr(n) of genome actually read at epigenetic state (n, D_n); |Expr(n)| <= |D_n^{-1}(+)|; typical human somatic cell expresses ~5000 of ~20000 genes.

name: Potency Restriction
summary: Monotone relationship: as refinement level n increases, |D_n^{-1}(+)| decreases; five potency levels correspond to progressively restrictive chromatin partitions.

name: Intergenerational Transfer
summary: Epigenetic mark propagation via DNMT1 (~95% fidelity per CpG per division) and histone read-write mechanisms (PRC2, SET1); inherently lossy SelfDesc closure.

name: Waddington Landscape
summary: Refinement tower (0,D_0) -> ... -> (k,D_k) with defect functional surface; active gene count non-increasing; valleys = stable cell types, ridges = barriers between fates.

name: Cell Fate
summary: Terminal epigenetic state (k, D_k) at bottom of Waddington landscape; fully restricted chromatin (unipotent), expressing only specialized-function genes.

name: Epigenetic Drift
summary: Age-related loss of epigenetic fidelity: methylation erosion, histone noise, chromatin boundary blur; Horvath clock (~350 CpG sites) quantifies drift empirically.

name: Differentiation Is Irreversible
summary: Under normal conditions, descent through Waddington landscape is irreversible; SelfDesc maintenance (DNMT1, histone propagation) preserves restriction but does not reverse it.

name: Reprogramming as Refinement Reversal
summary: Controlled reversal of refinement tower is structurally possible; Yamanaka factors (Oct4, Sox2, Klf4, c-Myc) override maintenance machinery and reopen silenced chromatin.

name: Drift Bounded by Repair
summary: Epigenetic maintenance bounded by repair budget; while funded, maintenance enzymes restore marks with bounded error; budget exhaustion causes uncontrolled drift in late life.

name: Cell Fate as Fixed Point
summary: Terminal differentiation is a fixed point of evaluator dynamics; SelfDesc closure restores partition after small deviations; evaluator applied to state reproduces the state.

name: Neural Defect Level
summary: Four hierarchical levels of neural defect: (1) molecular (misfolding, aggregation), (2) synaptic (loss, depletion), (3) circuit (pathway degradation), (4) network (connectivity loss).

name: Neural Defect Tower
summary: 4-level defect accumulation structure with one functional Delta_i(n) per level; each monotonically non-decreasing; levels cascade when threshold theta_i is crossed.

name: Neurodegenerative Disease Mapping
summary: Each neurodegenerative disease characterized by dominant defect level: Alzheimer's = Level 1, Parkinson's = Level 3, ALS = Level 3, Huntington's = Level 1, normal aging = all levels sub-threshold.

name: Neural Hayflick Bound
summary: H_i = R_max(i)/r_i per level; overall cognitive bound H_neural = min(H_1,...,H_4); geometric decay rate (1-iota_tau)^n governs baseline.

name: Inter-Level Cascade
summary: Level i defect beyond threshold theta_i triggers accelerated accumulation at Level i+1; cascade is unidirectional (upward): molecular -> synaptic -> circuit -> network.

name: Neural Defect Monotonicity
summary: At each level i, defect functional Delta_i(n) is monotonically non-decreasing; total neural defect sum inherits monotonicity from VI.D43.

name: Neurodegeneration = Hayflick Crossing
summary: Neurodegenerative disease occurs when level-specific Hayflick bound H_i exhausted before organismal limit; repair budget depleted, defects cross cascade threshold irreversibly.

name: Sleep Repair Function
summary: Maps nested sleep lemniscate lobes to defect tower levels: NREM/SWS -> Level 1 repair (glymphatic clearance), REM -> Level 2 repair (synaptic homeostasis); Levels 3-4 not repaired.

name: Sleep Consolidates Levels 1-2
summary: Sleep lobe implements defect consolidation at Levels 1-2 of neural defect tower; NREM activates glymphatic clearance, REM activates synaptic homeostasis; Levels 3-4 accumulate irreversibly.

name: Sleep Deprivation Accelerates Defect Crossing
summary: Chronic sleep deprivation skips Level 1-2 repair cycles, accelerating budget exhaustion; Level 1 threshold crossed earlier, triggering cascade to Level 2 degradation.

name: Substrate Abstraction
summary: Five tau-Distinction and three SelfDesc conditions are jointly necessary and sufficient for life, independent of material substrate; no condition references material predicates.

name: Scope: structural, not empirical
summary: Substrate Abstraction is structural: proves conditions impose no substrate restriction; does not predict which systems will satisfy them. Carbon sufficient not necessary.

name: Constructive Consciousness Criteria
summary: Three testable conditions: CC1 (consumer-sector topology with two independent recurrent loops), CC2 (Eval^(2) loop closed), CC3 (structural self-model maintained). All required.

name: Consciousness Criteria Equivalence
summary: CC1-CC3 equivalent to minimal conscious agent (VI.D69); CC1 <-> D69(i), CC2 <-> D69(iii), CC3 <-> D68; substrate-independent criteria.

name: Silicon Realization
summary: No structural obstruction to non-biological consciousness; each CC1-CC3 can in principle be satisfied by non-carbon systems; possibility proof, not prediction.

name: Empirical identification caveat
summary: Whether a specific system satisfies CC1-CC3 is empirical, outside tau-framework scope; framework provides criterion, identification requires empirical investigation.

name: Current AI systems
summary: No claim about current AI systems satisfying CC1-CC3; most lack CC1 entirely (no genuine mixed-sector feedback topology with two independent recurrent loops).
